title: Tetrataenium cardiocarpum
intro: Tetrataenium cardiocarpum is a plant species belonging to the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. Traditionally, Tetrataenium cardiocarpum is used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tetrataenium cardiocarpum include sesquiterpenes, phenolic compounds, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Tetrataenium cardiocarpum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are used in traditional remedies, such as teas, tinctures, and ointments, to treat various health conditions.
